attributes.Comments: Slide is undermining road on both sides of road.  Road traffic is destabilising slide.

Top section slope angle 52 deg.  3 x 8 m.
Bottom section slope angle 42 deg.  18 x 30 m.
Top of slide is covered by vegetated overhang.
Headscarp 0.5 m high by 47 m wide.  Actual size of slip unknown.
Wet gally has formed down centre of failure.
Top of slope angle 62 deg and 10 m long.  Bottom of slope angle 84 deg and 3 m long.
Seal on road has been repaired but slumping has continued.
Soil has a high peat fraction.
Could cause damming of Blackwater Creek.
Note that headscarp is 20 m wide.
Large earthquake triggered slide at the head of a steep sided gully.

Layered sandstone. limestone & coal.
Orientation Comment: Exposed face has numerous slides across 300 m extent.  Average slope angle given.
Exposed face has numerous slides across 400 m extent.  Average slope angle given.
Needs regular cleaning from roadway and slope base drain.
Unable to get close enough for detailed data.  Roadway has slumped - movement is probably on-going.
Preferential erosion along soft rock layers with soil covering.
Large area deforested by slide.  Minimal growth visible.
Undercutting of soil horizons.  Weathering is severe.  Concretions (SW-2-179-2-2) are found in grain size differentiated layers of Mudstone (HW-6-224-1-6).  As talus blocks drain. its removed.
Looks like a reactivated ancient debris flow.
Looks like a reactivated ancient debris flow.
Debris on other side of road has been removed from road.  Depth of debris is 1 m.
Base of slip at 2433200E 5931900N.  Large slip which blocked the Buller during the 1969? Earthquake.
Very bare. partially vegetated landslide scar.  Further activity possible.
Slope is mainly alluvion with recent talus on edge of Upper Buller River.
Foliation on rock blocks is 046/46SE.  Blocks of 1 cu m isolated on a plane parallel to sub parallel to slope.  Unable to check dimensions.
Single slope but with two talus aprons altering fall trajectory.
Revegitated slip that has been terraced (without visible support) for habitation.  Unable to obtain dimensions of failure surface.

Weathering penetrates to 4 m depth.  River rounded boulders and pebbles are sandwiched between layers of siltstone and mudstone.
Rock Type also includes CW-VW-143-1-6.
Site requires evacuation of rockfall material due to proximity to roadway.
Sandstone/ siltstone jointed at 200 mm to 2 m.
Talus has to be removed to clear roadway.

Limestone. sandstone and coal seams.
Orientation Comment: Slope angle and erosion rate varies between layers of coal. sandstone and limestone.
Includes coal seam.  Overlain by 1st (coal is under cutting - preferential weathering).
Gravels (unconsolidated).  Coordinates indicate centre of feature.
Rock type classified as soil due to degree of weathering.
Debris contain some large boulders (1 m diameter +) which poses very real threat to State Highway 6 SE of Westport.
Block isolation is occuring due to groundwater and precipitation percolating through open joints.
Fresh activity in places.  Photo shows typical outcrop.
Large single slip and rockfall possibly earthquake triggered.
Sandstone is almost completely weathered to soil in outer 300 mm but cohesive enough to be regarded as rock

Schmitd # 12 to 35 (dependant on weathering).
Other rock type MW-S-192-2-3
Slip was evacuated by the NZ Army. using blasting and mechanical excavation.  Major tourist track.

Building is still occuring in run out zone.  Complex failure includes rockfall and rock topple in limestone. plus debris flow.  Rockfall and rapid debris flow hazard area notified in BDC District Plan Maps B4 & C2.

Failure occurred just prior to visit.
Contains 1 m diameter boulders and  very active drainage channels.  Threat to road extensive.  Active slurry flows observed.
Orientation Comment: Slope angle varies from 49 deg at south side to 55 deg at north side.
This landslide would have originally blocked the Mawheraiti River & was possibly triggered by river flow.
Slope has been cut back. shaved and drainage pipes emplaced.
Also contains alternating beds of coal and mudstone.

attributes.Descriptio: Minor washout reported by Farmer at Br6. 2.6km Rapahoe Line during high river level. Repairs completed 11:00 02/01/05 using digger. No train services affected.
LINE CLOSURE TRACK SUBSIDENCE STILLWATER - GREYMOUTH. Bridge 114 350mm movement within 10kmh permanent speed restricted area. Track clearance 1015hrs 26/01/05.
Struck slip digger dispatched to site.Services suspended pending slip clearance.

AC Transfield Services reported slip located 1000hrs during 155 logged track inspection. Suspected service 841 struck small slip Train Controller imposed 10kmh speed restriction process under investigation.
Track formation subsided during thrusting of new underpass at 8.32km between Greymouth and Hokitika. X6 stopped and polited over site by Gang. Work being performed under Foul Time.
Slip Buller Gorge located by wet weather track inspection.

845 STRUCK SLIP BULLER GORGE. Loco Engineer made emergency brake application travelling on 25kmh speed restriction and reported collision with small slip & tree with further movement on hillside. service set back clear. Severe Weather warning in force.
803 Stopped at 198.5km due to flooding/washout. Significant washout found at 201.3km MDLND Line. 803 set back to Stillwater to offload passengers.
WASHOUT BRIDGE 23 SWL. severe weather conditions track clearance received 1625 hours. delay 83214 130 minutes.
WASHOUT BRIDGE END RED JACKS - AHAURA bridge 23 Severe Weather conditions line closed pending repairs.
FLASH FLOOD BRIDGE 148 GRANITY. reported by NZ Police debris cleared by Ganger track clearance 1506 hours 80 minutes.
LE 841 Reported Slip at Tunnel 1 Reefton-Tawhai. On inspection AM 6/10 was decided to close line.
Wt 82 hit slip in Landing Cutting at 84.5km SWL. Speedo on DC4133 damaged. WT82 able to proceed.
LINE CLOSURE - SUBSIDENCE approximately 30 metres long by 10-15 metres deep.
Damage to bridge & Abutment at Rocky point at 141.63 caused by washout
SWL Closed Due To Slip. Line reopened 1020hrs to allow passage of 845.829. Work continuing between trains
Tawhai Tunnel closed to all services conseq on slip movement at tunnel mouth
Washout 6 sleepers in length. right hand rail in mid-air approx 2 meter gap below rail next to Gray River. Line closed until clearance receieved.
SLIP DETECTOR ACTIVATED wait inspection delay 850.

Emergency call from 803 .
Train had just passed over a slip.

Wahout between 182.5km & 183.30km between Moana & Kokiri
about 5m long and 10m deep.
Line Closure - Subsidence expected clearance Friday.
A culvert clogged with debris  causing problem. Track Closed from 1450 to 1910hrs.
Slip buller gorge at 119km found by gang on wet weather run
large debris flow from high on Mt Turiwhate. it covered highway up to 2m and for length of 8m
